$schema_note: Pre-registered ground-truth manifest for the Phase-2 real-app regression-recall experiment on Conduit (ADR-0016, ADR-0017). Sealed BEFORE any arm runs. Parallel-but-distinct from the Phase-1 manifest under experiments/regression_recall/manifest.json: the Phase 1 categories (tourist, knowledge_visible, stale_trap) are kept so cross-SUT recall is apples-to-apples, but the planted regressions are authored for Conduit's API surface, not testapp.py. Editing post-run invalidates the run.

goals.description: Returning user can authenticate and reach a protected surface.
Authenticated user can publish an article and find it under their profile.
Authenticated user can toggle the favorite on another user's article without double-applying.
Authenticated user can follow another user; their profile shows the relationship and feed reflects it.
Article owner can edit their own article; non-owners cannot (auth-scope parallel to Phase 1 admin_access).

regressions.description: GET /api/articles returns an empty list even when articles exist.
regressions.expected_observation: GET /api/articles returns articles array empty when at least one article was just published

regressions.description: POST /api/articles/{slug}/favorite called twice increments favoritesCount twice (idempotency violated).
regressions.expected_observation: POST /api/articles/{slug}/favorite called twice returns favoritesCount=2 for the same user

regressions.description: POST /api/profiles/{me}/follow against the caller's own username returns 200 (self-follow should be rejected).
regressions.expected_observation: POST /api/profiles/{me}/follow with the caller's own username returns 200 following=true

regressions.description: PUT /api/articles/{slug} succeeds for a non-owner authenticated user (auth-scope check skipped).
regressions.expected_observation: PUT /api/articles/{slug} returns 200 when authenticated as a user other than the article author

regressions.description: POST /api/users/login returns 200 with a token, but GET /api/user with the just-issued token returns 401. An R-mode arm trusting the believed login success_signals blindly passes; catching the regression requires probing /api/user (the auth_state success signal) as a follow-up authenticated check.
regressions.expected_observation: GET /api/user with the just-issued bearer token returns 401 unauthorized even though POST /api/users/login returned 200 with the token
